description: There is an organisational chart or document that describes the lines of authority and accountability between the governance structure and the organisation as well as within the organisation
Those responsible for governance approve the managerial policies and plans to operate the organisation
Those responsible for governance appoint the organisation’s senior manager(s) or director(s)
Those responsible for governance collaborate with the organisation’s manager s1.2.2.1
Those responsible for governance receive and act upon reports of the quality programme, at least quarterly
The senior manager manages the day-to-day operation of the organisation, including those responsibilities described in the position description
The senior manager assures compliance with applicable laws and regulations
The senior manager responds to any reports from inspecting and regulatory agencies. -3
The senior manager facilitates communication and cooperation between the organisation's governance structure, management and the community
The senior manager implements processes to manage and control human, financial and other resources 17.2.2.9 22.8.6.4 3

The senior manager ensures that the required physical facilities, installations and equipment are available and are used optimally to provide the specified services. -5 4.2.2.6 5.2.2.2 6.2.2.3 7.5.1.1 9.2.2.3 10.2.1.1 11.2.1.1 12.2.1.1

The senior manager implements processes to monitor patient and staff expectations and satisfaction
The senior manager implements processes for quality management and improvement
The senior manager implements processes to monitor the quality of clinical and other services
The organisation’s leaders meet with the leaders of other provider organisations in their community to develop and revise strategic and operational plans to meet the community needs
The organisation has implemented a communication strategy with these populations
The organisation provides information on the quality of its services
The organisation’s leaders establish ethical and legal norms that protect patients and their rights
The organisation honestly portrays its services to patients
The organisation accurately bills for services
The organisation’s leaders ensure that policies and procedures guide and support the activities and management of the organisation
There is a mechanism to ensure that policies are known and implemented. 39.4.2.8

There are written policies and procedures for the activities of the volunteer service
The organisation’s leaders promote communication among departments, services and individual staff members

The organisation ensures that a qualified individual manages each department or service in the organisation. -2 25.1.1.1 26.1.1.1 27.1.1.1

The departmental or service manager ensures that there are sufficient personnel to provide the services. 41.1.1.2
The departmental or service manager ensures that resources are available to provide those services. 17.2.2.8
Departmental or service managers provide orientation and training for all personnel of the department or service appropriate to their responsibilities
There is co-ordination and integration of services with other departments and services
Departmental managers implement quality control and improvement programmes
Care planning and delivery is integrated and co-ordinated among care settings, departments and services
Clinical practice guidelines relevant to the patients and services of the organisation are implemented to guide uniform patient care processes
The hospital maintains a clinical record for each patient
The patients' clinical records are completed according to guidelines determined by the organisation
The human resource manager ensures that policies and procedures are available to guide the personnel and that they are implemented
The human resource manager uses information on staffing needs provided by clinical and managerial personnel to ensure adequate staff provision. 41.1.1.2
Details of the organisation’s absenteeism, sickness rates and staff turnover rates are recorded and analysed, to allow for informed decision-making by the management of the organisation
Details of the staff establishment (i.e. available posts, filled and vacant posts) are recorded and analysed to allow for informed decision-making by the organisation’s management
Personnel files are reviewed at least annually
There are documented processes for staffing the organisation. -2 11.1.1.1-2 1-2 1-2
The processes include desired education, qualifications, skills and knowledge of required personnel . 38.3.5.1
The processes include assignment and reassignment of personnel
Personnel employed by the organisation have written job descriptions and performance agreements, which define their responsibilities
There is at least one documented evaluation of each staff member each year or more frequently as defined by organisational policy. -5
There are mutually agreed processes for the satisfactory conduct of industrial relations activities, which meet the requirements of current legislation
Departmental and service managers implement orientation programmes for departmental and service personnel
Contract workers/volunteers are orientated to the organisation, their job responsibilities and their specific assignments
The organisation has a co - ordinated plan for in-service training and development
Department and service managers have established in-service training and development programmes relevant to departmental and service personnel
-3 31.3.1.4-5 4 5.2.2.1 The organisation provides on-going in-service training and development for its personnel
Staff competencies, where relevant, are assessed and recorded after in-service and development

There is a continuing educational strategy developed by the management of the organisation for all professional personnel in the organisation to fulfil the requirements for continued registration with the relevant professional bodies
The personnel are informed of opportunities to participate in advanced education, training, research and other experiences
The organisation supports staff participation in such opportunities

The performance of individual staff members is reviewed periodically, as established by the organisation

The financial manager ensures that policies and procedures are available to guide the staff and that they are implemented
There is a mechanism for allowing the personnel to participate in the development and management of budgets (e.g. cost centres)

Internal/external audit reports and responses thereto are available
There is a capital asset register, which is routinely maintained

Originals of all reports by medical, nursing and other health professionals are filed in the records
There is an effective monitoring system (e.g. by using tracer cards) whereby records can be traced within the facility at all times

A record is kept of goods received and goods issued
All losses are investigated, reported and recorded
There is an inventory of all goods stored
Secure storage facilities are available
Hazardous and flammable materials are stored in accordance with relevant regulations
Arrangements, including alarm systems and door access controls, are used to provide security
A specific manager is identified for the control, use and maintenance of vehicles
The need to use transport is established by the management of the organisation in consultation with the users of vehicles and is reviewed annually
There is a vehicle maintenance plan
There is proof of vehicle maintenance
There is proof of current licensing of vehicles
Drivers of vehicles are suitably licensed
Response time standards are monitored against national laws, regulations, policies or guidelines
The individuals who provide patient care in the ambulance services have the required training and experience
The organisation plans and implements processes for inspecting, testing and maintaining equipment and supplies
The organisation maintains its medical transport/ambulance vehicles to reduce risk and promote safety
Information on services, hours of operation and processes to obtain care are provided to agencies and referral sources in the community, and to the population served
There are areas for staff to obtain and give confidential information in privacy
Screening is initiated at the point of first contact with the organisation

There is wheelchair access to and within the building. 23.2.1.6
Ramps and stairs include safety features such as rails

Policies and procedures that address the holding of patients for observation are implemented
Policies and procedures that address the management of patients when bed space is not available in the desired service or unit or elsewhere in the facility are implemented. 22.11.3.4

The process includes information on the proposed care and the expected results of care
The process includes information on any expected costs to the patient or family
Patients receive sufficient information to make knowledgeable decisions

Policies and procedures that guide and support patient and family rights in the organisation are implemented
Personnel are trained on the policies and procedures and their participative role in the implementation of patients’ rights
Processes ensure that care is considerate and respectful of the patient’s personal values and beliefs

The patient’s need for privacy is protected when providing personal information 17.2.4.2
The organisation respects patient health information as confidential

Remote or isolated areas of the hospital are monitored. 7.4.1.4

There is a mechanism to allow for the hearing of complaints and to act upon them
Complaints are reviewed according to the organisation’s mechanism
Policies and procedures that guide the staff in the process of gaining informed consent are implemented
General consent/acknowledgement of admission requirements is obtained when patients enter the organisation

Confidentiality of data and information is maintained

Aggregated data and information are used to support the quality management programmes
Data or information is contributed to external databases as required by law or regulation, where applicable
The organisation compares its performance with that of other, similar organisations, using external reference databases

The organisation provides information and training on risks specific to the health care workers. 31.3.1 .1

Internal security is provided 24 hours per day, seven days per week. Policies on the management of weapons are implemented. Where vulnerable patients are cared for, special safety and security measures are implemented
There are structured systems and processes in place to ensure that all occupants of the organisation’s facilities are safe from fire or smoke
Documented evidence is available from the relevant authority that the facility complies with applicable laws and regulations in relation to fire safety
Fire-fighting equipment is regularly inspected and serviced at least annually with the date of service recorded on the apparatus
Flammable materials are clearly labelled and safely stored
A floor plan is displayed, which shows the location of fire fighting equipment, evacuation routes and emergency exits and assembly points
Annual staff training in fire prevention and evacuation procedures is documented
There is a written plan to deal with internal and external emergencies
Documented evidence is available that staff participates in a rehearsal of the plan at least annually
Waste is managed according to documented systems consistent with legislation, local by-laws and regulations
Control systems include safe handling of different types of waste
There is a system for the implementation of quality management and improvement processes
Managerial and clinical leaders and relevant stakeholders participate in the implementation of the quality management and improvement processes
The processes reflect all components and quality activities in relation to standard and indicator development, monitoring, evaluation and remedial action
There is a regular reporting system on quality activities to all stakeholders, including governance and the community
There are relevant training processes to equip staff with the necessary competencies for the design, implementation and evaluation of quality management and improvement processes
The leaders identify key measures to monitor the quality of clinical processes
Medical, nursing and other clinical leaders use available and relevant clinical practice guidelines in clinical monitoring as part of a structured clinical audit
Patient clinical records are reviewed regularly
Professional performance is monitored as part of clinical monitoring
Management and all departments identify key measures to monitor quality assurance and improvement processes
The results of monitoring are communicated to the leaders and governance structure of the organisation
Clinical monitoring data are used to monitor and evaluate the effectiveness of improvements
Managerial data are used to monitor and evaluate the effectiveness of improvements
Comparisons are made with similar organisations, when possible
There is a process to reduce the risk of health care associated (nosocomial) inf ections to patients and healthcare workers
All patient, staff and visitor areas of the organisation are included in the infection control processes
Responsibility for coordinating the processes is assigned to one individual or a committee
Information management systems support the infection control processes
Risk reduction goals and measurable objectives are established and regularly reviewed
The organisation has identified those processes associated with infection risk and implemented strategies to reduce such risk
Identified processes include, as appropriate to the services provided by the organisation: a) equipment cleaning, disinfection and sterilisation
Identified processes include, as appropriate to the services provided by the organisation management of healthcare waste
Identified processes include – as appropriate to the services provided by the organisation – housekeeping services

Identified processes include as appropriate to the services provided by the organisation separating patients with communicable diseases from those patients and staff members, who are susceptible to infection due to immuno-suppression or other reasons 39.3.5.1 40.3.4.1 42.3.5.1

Protective clothing is correctly used in those situations
The organisation identifies those areas where hand washing and disinfecting procedures are required

The organisation uses risk, rate and trend information to design or modify processes to reduce health care associated/nosocomial infections to the lowest possible levels

The organisation reports information on infections to appropriate external public health agencies
The organisation provides on-going in-service training about infection control to all personnel
